sorting_by_multiple_columns.label: Sorting by Multiple Columns
sorting_by_multiple_columns.summary: Learn how primary and secondary sorting work, and how to sort text, numbers, and dates using more than one column.

sketch2.title: How do you sort text with multiple columns?

sketch3.title: How do you sort numbers and dates with multiple columns?

primary_sort.title: The primary sort decides the first main order of the result.
primary_sort.bodyMarkdown:
When you sort by more than one column, the first column listed is the **primary sort**.

SQL uses it first to organize the rows.

Code example:

~~~sql
SELECT name, category
FROM products
ORDER BY category, name;
~~~

Here, `category` is the primary sort because it appears first.

So the key idea is:

> the first sort column has the strongest control over the order

### Try it yourself

Look at this query:

~~~sql
SELECT name, stock, price
FROM products
ORDER BY stock, price;
~~~

Ask yourself:

- which column controls the main order?
- what would SQL look at first?
- which values would be grouped together first?

secondary_sort.title: The secondary sort breaks ties inside the primary sort groups.
secondary_sort.bodyMarkdown:
If two or more rows have the same value in the primary sort column, SQL can use another column to decide their order.

That second column is the **secondary sort**.

Code example:

~~~sql
SELECT name, category
FROM products
ORDER BY category, name;
~~~

If several rows share the same `category`, SQL uses `name` to order those tied rows.

So the idea is:

> the second sort column helps order rows that tie on the first one

### Try it yourself

Read this query:

~~~sql
SELECT name, created_at
FROM products
ORDER BY created_at, name;
~~~

Ask yourself:

- what happens if two rows have the same `created_at` value?
- which column breaks the tie?
- why does that make the result easier to read?

sorting_text.title: Sorting text with more than one column is useful for grouped labels and names.
sorting_text.bodyMarkdown:
Text sorting is useful when you want grouped labels first, then readable order inside each group.

For example, you might sort by:

- category first
- name second

That gives a result where categories form groups, and names are neatly ordered inside each group.

Code example:

~~~sql
SELECT name, category
FROM products
ORDER BY category ASC, name ASC;
~~~

This groups rows by category, then sorts names inside each category group.

So the big idea is:

> text sorting can be layered to create grouped, readable results

### Try it yourself

Look at this order:

- `category` first
- `name` second

Ask yourself:

- what kind of grouping will appear?
- what will happen inside one category group?
- why is this clearer than sorting by `name` alone?

sorting_numbers_and_dates.title: Numbers and dates can also be sorted in levels.
sorting_numbers_and_dates.bodyMarkdown:
Multiple-column sorting is not only for text.

You can also sort by:

- one number, then another number
- a date, then a name
- a category, then a date

Code example:

~~~sql
SELECT name, stock, price
FROM products
ORDER BY stock ASC, price ASC;
~~~

This sorts by `stock` first, then uses `price` when stock values tie.

Another example:

~~~sql
SELECT name, created_at
FROM products
ORDER BY created_at ASC, name ASC;
~~~

This sorts by date first, then by name.

So the idea is:

> multi-column sorting works for numbers and dates too

### Try it yourself

Read this query:

~~~sql
SELECT name, category, created_at
FROM products
ORDER BY category, created_at;
~~~

Ask yourself:

- which column creates the first level of order?
- what happens inside one category group?
- why might this be useful for reading grouped timelines?

m3_s02_sort_category_then_name.title: Primary and secondary text sort
m3_s02_sort_category_then_name.prompt:
The SQLite workspace already has a table named `products`.

Write a query that shows the `name` and `category` columns, sorted first by `category` and then by `name`.

Then click **Run**.

help.concept: When sorting by more than one column, the first column is the primary sort and the second column breaks ties.
help.hint_1: Select `name` and `category`, then sort by `category` first and `name` second.
help.hint_2: Write a two-column query on `products`, then add an `ORDER BY` clause with the grouping column first and the tie-breaking text column second.

m3_s02_sort_category_then_name.hint: Put the primary sort first and the secondary sort second.
m3_s02_sort_category_then_name.starterCode: -- Show products sorted by category, then name

m3_s02_sort_category_then_price_desc.title: Group by category, then highest price first
m3_s02_sort_category_then_price_desc.prompt:
The `products` table already exists.

Write a query that shows the `name`, `category`, and `price` columns, sorted first by `category` and then by `price` in descending order.

Then run it.

help.concept: A secondary sort can use a different direction from the primary sort.
help.hint_1: Select the three requested columns, then sort by `category` first and `price` second.
help.hint_2: Build a three-column query on `products`, then add sorting so rows are grouped by category first, and inside each category group the higher prices appear before the lower ones.

m3_s02_sort_category_then_price_desc.hint: Use a primary text sort and a descending numeric secondary sort.
m3_s02_sort_category_then_price_desc.starterCode: -- Show products sorted by category, then price descending

m3_s02_sort_stock_then_price.title: Sort by one number, then another
m3_s02_sort_stock_then_price.prompt:
The `products` table already exists.

Write a query that shows the `name`, `stock`, and `price` columns, sorted first by `stock` ascending and then by `price` ascending.

Then click **Run**.

help.concept: Multiple number columns can be used together in sorting just like text columns.
help.hint_1: Select the three requested columns, then sort by `stock` first and `price` second.
help.hint_2: Write a three-column query on `products`, then add sorting so stock controls the main order and price breaks ties inside matching stock values.

m3_s02_sort_stock_then_price.hint: Use stock as primary sort and price as secondary sort.
m3_s02_sort_stock_then_price.starterCode: -- Show products sorted by stock, then price

m3_s02_sort_created_at_then_name.title: Sort by date, then name
m3_s02_sort_created_at_then_name.prompt:
The `products` table already exists.

Write a query that shows the `name` and `created_at` columns, sorted first by `created_at` ascending and then by `name` ascending.

Then run it.

help.concept: Dates can be the primary sort, with text used to break ties inside the same date.
help.hint_1: Select `name` and `created_at`, then sort by the date column first.
help.hint_2: Build a two-column query on `products`, then add sorting so earlier dates come first and names break ties inside matching dates.

m3_s02_sort_created_at_then_name.starterCode: -- Show products sorted by created date, then name

m3_s02_multi_sort_project.title: Practice sorting by multiple columns
m3_s02_multi_sort_project.prompt:
Use the SQL workspace on the right to practice multi-column sorting on the `products` table.

For each task:

1. type the query in the editor
2. click **Run**
3. read the **Results** tab
4. check which column controlled the main order and which column broke ties

Tasks:

1. Show `name` and `category` sorted by `category`, then `name`
2. Show `name`, `category`, and `price` sorted by `category`, then `price` descending
3. Show `name`, `stock`, and `price` sorted by `stock`, then `price`
4. Show `name` and `created_at` sorted by `created_at`, then `name`

Run one query at a time.
This project is about understanding primary and secondary sorting clearly.

help.concept: This project builds the habit of thinking in layers: main order first, tie-break order second.
help.hint_1: Before writing each query, decide which column should control the main grouping and which column should break ties.
help.hint_2: Write the `ORDER BY` columns in the same order as your logic: strongest sort first, tie-breaker second.

m3_s02_multi_sort_project.starterCode:
-- Task 1
-- Sort by category, then name

m3_s02_primary_sort_meaning.title: What is a primary sort?
m3_s02_primary_sort_meaning.prompt: When sorting by more than one column, what is the primary sort?

help.concept: The primary sort is the first sort rule SQL uses to arrange the result.
help.hint_2: It is the first column listed in the sort order and controls the main structure of the result.

options.a: The first sort column that controls the main order

m3_s02_secondary_sort_meaning.title: What is a secondary sort?
m3_s02_secondary_sort_meaning.prompt: When sorting by more than one column, what is the secondary sort used for?

help.concept: The secondary sort helps order rows that tie on the primary sort.
help.hint_2: It is used after the first sort column when rows share the same primary value.

options.a: It breaks ties inside the primary sort groups
options.b: It replaces the primary sort completely

m3_s02_sorting_text_meaning.title: How does multi-column text sorting help?
m3_s02_sorting_text_meaning.prompt: Why is sorting text with more than one column useful?

help.concept: Multi-column text sorting helps create readable grouped results.
help.hint_1: Think grouped labels, then readable order inside each group.
help.hint_2: One text column can create the groups, and another can organize rows within those groups.

options.a: It can group rows by one text column and organize them by another
options.b: It removes the need for ORDER BY

m3_s02_sorting_numbers_dates_meaning.title: How do numbers and dates fit into multi-column sorting?
m3_s02_sorting_numbers_dates_meaning.prompt: What is true about sorting numbers and dates with multiple columns?

help.concept: Numbers and dates can be sorted in layers just like text columns.
help.hint_2: A number or date column can control the main order, and another column can break ties.

m3_s02_sorting_numbers_dates_meaning.hint: Think layered sorting works there too.

options.a: They can also be used in multi-column sorting
options.b: Only text columns can be sorted in multiple levels
options.d: Numbers remove the need for a secondary sort

m3_s02_check_multi_sort_result.title: How should you check a multi-sort result?
m3_s02_check_multi_sort_result.prompt: After running a multi-column sort query, what is a strong habit?

help.concept: You should check both the main grouping and the tie-break ordering.
help.hint_1: Think first column order, then second column order inside ties.
help.hint_2: Do not only check the first sort. Also verify whether rows with the same first value are ordered correctly by the second value.

m3_s02_check_multi_sort_result.hint: Check the main order and the tie-breaks.

options.a: Check both the primary order and the tie-break order
options.d: Assume the second sort worked without checking

m3_s02_primary_secondary_true.title: What is true about primary and secondary sorting?
m3_s02_primary_secondary_true.prompt: Select all true statements about primary and secondary sorting.

help.concept: The first sort column controls the main order, and later sort columns break ties.
help.hint_1: Think strongest sort first, tie-breaker second.
help.hint_2: Choose the statements about layered sorting logic.

options.a: The first sort column is the primary sort
options.b: The second sort column can break ties
options.c: The primary sort has stronger control over the result order
options.d: The secondary sort always replaces the primary sort

m3_s02_text_sort_true.title: What is true about sorting text with multiple columns?
m3_s02_text_sort_true.prompt: Select all true statements about multi-column text sorting.

help.concept: Multi-column text sorting can create grouped and readable results.
help.hint_2: Choose the statements about layered text ordering.

options.a: One text column can create groups
options.b: Another text column can order rows inside those groups
options.d: Text columns cannot be sorted together
options.e: Category then name is a common example

m3_s02_number_date_sort_true.title: What is true about sorting numbers and dates?
m3_s02_number_date_sort_true.prompt: Select all true statements about sorting numbers and dates with more than one column.

help.concept: Numbers and dates can be layered in sorting just like text columns.
help.hint_1: Think stock then price, or date then name.
help.hint_2: Choose the statements about using numeric or date columns in multi-sort logic.

m3_s02_number_date_sort_true.hint: Think layered number and date sorting.

options.a: A number column can be the primary sort
options.b: A date column can be the primary sort
options.c: Another column can break ties after a number or date sort
options.d: Numbers and dates cannot be used in multi-column sorting
options.e: Date then name is one example of layered sorting

m3_s02_multi_sort_benefits_true.title: What are the benefits of multi-column sorting?
m3_s02_multi_sort_benefits_true.prompt: Select all true statements about why multi-column sorting is useful.

help.concept: Multi-column sorting creates more precise and more readable result order.
help.hint_2: Choose the statements about more controlled result ordering.

options.a: It helps organize results more precisely
options.b: It helps break ties in a controlled way
options.c: It can make grouped results easier to read
options.d: It removes the need to check result order
options.e: It helps when one sort column alone is not enough

m3_s02_multi_sort_workflow_true.title: What matches the right workflow?
m3_s02_multi_sort_workflow_true.prompt: Select all statements that match a good workflow for multi-column sorting.

help.concept: A good workflow is to decide the strongest sort first, choose a tie-breaker second, then verify both.
help.hint_1: Think strongest sort, tie-breaker, result check.
help.hint_2: Choose the steps that help translate layered ordering into the query.

m3_s02_multi_sort_workflow_true.hint: Choose the main sort, then the tie-breaker.

options.a: Decide which column should control the main order
options.b: Choose another column to break ties if needed
options.c: Write the sort columns in that logic order
options.d: Run the query and verify both levels of ordering

m3_s02_reorder_primary_sort_logic.title: Primary sort
m3_s02_reorder_primary_sort_logic.prompt: Reorder the ideas to explain the primary sort.

help.concept: The first sort column controls the main order of the result.
help.hint_2: It has the strongest effect on the result order.

tokens.t1: the first sort column is the primary sort
tokens.t2: it controls the main structure of the result
tokens.t3: it has the strongest effect on row order

help.concept: The second sort column breaks ties inside the first sort groups.
help.hint_2: Then think tie-breaker.

tokens.t1: some rows can tie on the first sort column
tokens.t2: the second sort column is used after that
tokens.t3: it breaks ties inside the primary sort groups

m3_s02_reorder_text_sort_layers.title: Layered text sorting
m3_s02_reorder_text_sort_layers.prompt: Reorder the ideas to explain text sorting with multiple columns.

help.concept: Text sorting can create groups first and readable order inside them.
help.hint_2: Then name inside each group.

tokens.t1: one text column can create groups
tokens.t2: another text column can order rows inside those groups
tokens.t3: the result becomes more grouped and readable

m3_s02_reorder_number_date_layers.title: Layered number and date sorting
m3_s02_reorder_number_date_layers.prompt: Put the ideas in the best order.

help.concept: Numbers and dates can also be sorted in levels.
help.hint_2: Then tie-breaker.

tokens.t1: a number or date column can control the main order
tokens.t2: another column can break ties after that
tokens.t3: multi-column sorting works for numbers and dates too

m3_s02_reorder_multi_sort_workflow.title: Good multi-sort workflow
m3_s02_reorder_multi_sort_workflow.prompt: Reorder the workflow for multi-column sorting.

help.concept: A strong workflow is main sort first, tie-breaker second, then verify both.
help.hint_2: Result checking comes after.

tokens.t1: decide which column should control the main order
tokens.t2: choose another column to break ties if needed
tokens.t3: run the query and verify both levels of ordering

m3_s02_fill_primary_keyword.title: Primary sort meaning
m3_s02_fill_primary_keyword.prompt: Fill in the blank with the best word.

help.concept: The primary sort is the first sort rule.
help.hint_2: The answer is first.

m3_s02_fill_primary_keyword.template: The primary sort is the ____ sort column listed.

m3_s02_fill_secondary_keyword.title: Secondary sort meaning
m3_s02_fill_secondary_keyword.prompt: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.

help.concept: The secondary sort is used to break ties.
help.hint_2: The missing word is ties.

m3_s02_fill_secondary_keyword.template: The secondary sort is mainly used to break ____.

m3_s02_fill_text_example.title: Text sorting example
m3_s02_fill_text_example.prompt: Fill in the blank with the best column name.

help.concept: Category is a common text column used for grouping.
help.hint_2: The answer is category.

m3_s02_fill_text_example.template: A common first text sort column is ____.

m3_s02_fill_layered_sort_word.title: How multi-sort works
m3_s02_fill_layered_sort_word.prompt: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.

help.concept: Multi-column sorting works in levels.
help.hint_2: The word is levels.

m3_s02_fill_sort_strength.title: Strongest sort
m3_s02_fill_sort_strength.prompt: Fill in the blank with the best word.

help.concept: The first sort column has the strongest control over the result.
help.hint_2: The answer is strongest.

m3_s02_fill_sort_strength.template: The primary sort has the ____ control over the result order.
